What is APR in digital lending?.answer: APR is the effective annualised rate charged and is based on an all-inclusive cost and margin including cost of funds, credit cost and operating cost, processing fee, verification charges, maintenance charges, etc., and exclude contingent charges like penal charges, late payment charges, etc.
contexts: 2.1. Annual Percentage Rate (APR): APR is the effective annualised rate charged

What entities do the digital lending gui.answer: The digital lending guidelines apply to All Commercial Banks, Primary (Urban) Co-operative Banks, State Co-operative Banks, District Central Co-operative Banks, and Non-Banking Financial Companies (including Housing Finance Companies).
contexts: 1.1. All Commercial Banks, 1.2. Primary (Urban) Co-operative Banks, State Co-operative Banks, District Central Co-operative Banks; and 1.3. Non-Banking Financial Companies (including Housing Finance Companies)

What is a Digital Lending App (DLA)?.answer: A Digital Lending App (DLA) is a mobile and web-based application with a user interface that facilitates digital lending services.
contexts: 2.4. Digital Lending Apps/Platforms  (DLAs): Mobile and web- based applications with user interface that facilitate digital lending services.

Who is responsible if a lending service.answer: The Regulated Entity (RE) is responsible if a Lending Service Provider (LSP) violates rules, as the RE is required to ensure that the LSPs engaged by them comply with the guidelines.
contexts: REs shall ensure that any fees, charges, etc., payable to LSPs are paid directly by them (REs) and are not charged by LSP to the borrower directly.

Can loan disbursal be made to third-part.answer: No, loan disbursal shall always be made into the bank account of the borrower except for disbursals covered exclusively under statutory or regulatory mandate (of RBI or of any other regulator), flow of money between REs for co-lending transactions, and disbursals for specific end use, provided the loan is disbursed directly into the bank account of the end-beneficiary.
contexts: disbursements shall always be made into the bank account of the borrower except for disbursals covered exclusively under statutory or regulatory mandate (of RBI or of any other regulator) , flow of money between REs for c o-lending transactions 2 and disbursals for specific end use, provided the loan is disbursed directly into the bank account of the end-beneficiary.

Who should pay fees to lending service p.answer: REs (Regulated Entities) shall ensure that any fees, charges, etc., payable to LSPs are paid directly by them (REs) and are not charged by LSP to the borrower directly.
contexts: 4.1. Payment of Fees/Charges: REs shall ensure that any fees, charges, etc. , payable to LSPs are paid directly by them (REs) and are not charged by LSP to the borrower directly.

How should penal charges be calculated?.answer: Penal charges shall be based on the outstanding amount of the loan and the rate of such penal charges shall be disclosed upfront on an annualized basis to the borrower in the Key Fact Statement (KFS).
contexts: borrowers shall be based on the outstanding amount of the loan. Further, rate of such penal charges shall be disclosed upfront on an annualized basis to the borrower in the Key Fact Statement (KFS).

What must be disclosed in the Key Fact S.answer: The Key Fact Statement (KFS) shall contain the details of APR, the recovery mechanism, details of grievance redressal officer designated specifically to deal with digital lending/ FinTech related matter and other necessary information.
contexts: 5.2.2. The KFS shall, apart from other necessary information, contain the details of APR, the recovery mechanism, details of grievance redressal officer designated specifically to deal with digital lending/ FinTech related matter and
Any fees, charges, etc., which are not mentioned in the KFS cannot be charged by the REs to the borrower at any stage during the term of the loan.

What documents must be digitally provide.answer: Digitally signed documents such as Key Fact Statement (KFS), summary of loan product, sanction letter, terms and conditions, account statements, and privacy policies must be provided to borrowers.
contexts: 5.3. Digitally signed documents – REs shall ensure that digitally signed documents3 (on the letter head of the RE ) viz., KFS, summary of loan product, sanction letter, terms and conditions, account statements, privacy policies of the

What information must be displayed at on.answer: Information relating to the product features, loan limit and cost, etc.
contexts: at on- boarding/sign-up stage, prominently display information relating to the product features, loan limit and cost, etc., so as to make the borrowers aware of these aspects.

What happens if a borrower complaint is.answer: The borrower can lodge a complaint over the Complaint Management System (CMS) portal under the Reserve Bank-Integrated Ombudsman Scheme (RB-IOS) if the complaint is not resolved within the stipulated period, currently 30 days.
contexts: RE is not resolved by the RE within the stipulated period (currently 30 days), he/she can lodge a complaint over the Complaint Management System (CMS) portal under the Reserve Bank-Integrated Ombudsman Scheme (RB-IOS)
occupation, income, etc.), before extending any loan over their own DLAs and/or through LSPs engaged by them, with a view to assess ing the borrower’s creditworthiness in an auditable way.

Can credit limits be increased automatic.answer: No, credit limits cannot be increased automatically unless explicit consent of the borrower is taken on record for each such increase.
contexts: REs shall ensure that there is no automatic increase in credit limit unless explicit consent of borrower is taken on record for each such increase.

What happens during the cooling-off peri.answer: During the cooling-off period, a borrower shall be given an explicit option to exit digital loan by paying the principal and the proportionate APR without any penalty.
contexts: 8. Cooling off/look-up period –A borrower shall be given an explicit option to exit digital loan by paying the principal and the proportionate APR without any penalty during this period.
2.2. Cooling off/look-up period: A cooling off/ look-up period is the time window as determined by the Board of the RE which shall be given to borrowers for exiting
9.1. REs must conduct enhanced due diligence before entering into a partnership

Can DLAs access phone contacts or call l.answer: No, DLAs shall desist from accessing mobile phone resources like file and media, contact list, call logs, telephony functions.
contexts: DLAs shall desist from accessing mobile phone resources like file and media, contact list, call logs, telephony functions

What type of consent is required for dat.answer: Explicit consent of the borrower is required for data collection, with prior and explicit consent of the borrower having an audit trail.
contexts: 10.1. REs shall ensure that any collection of data by their DLAs and DLAs of their LSPs is need-based and with prior and explicit consent of the borrower having audit trail.
11.4. REs shall ensure that all data is stored only in servers located within India, while ensuring compliance with statutory obligations/ regulatory instructions.

Is biometric data allowed to be stored?.answer: No, unless allowed under extant statutory guidelines
contexts: 11.3. REs shall ensure that no biometric data is stored/ collected in the systems associated with the DLA of REs/ their LSPs, unless allowed under extant statutory guidelines.

What must be included in a privacy polic.answer: A comprehensive privacy policy must include details of third parties allowed to collect personal information, and must be compliant with applicable laws, associated regulations, and RBI guidelines.
contexts: 12. Comprehensive privacy policy

Are digital loans required to be reporte.answer: Yes, digital loans are required to be reported to Credit Information Companies (CICs).
contexts: 14.1. As per the provisions of the Credit Information Companies  (CIC) (Regulation) Act, 2005; CIC Rules, 2006; CIC Regulations, 2006 and related guidelines issued by RBI from time to time, REs shall ensure that any lending done through their DLAs and/or DLAs of LSPs is reported to CICs irrespective of its nature/ tenor.

What is FLDG in lending?.answer: First Loss Default Guarantee (FLDG) in which a third party guarantees a portion of the loan
